name: Apple iPad (10th Generation)
introText: The iPad 10th generation is the most affordable iPad with USB-C, a 10.9-inch Liquid Retina display, and 5G support — the best entry-level iPad at $449 CAD on Amazon.ca.
whatWorks: USB-C and modern design at the lowest iPad price — the 10th gen was the first base iPad with USB-C, ending the Lightning limitation and enabling faster data transfer and modern accessories.
10.9-inch display is the right size for most people — comfortable for video, reading, browsing, and light productivity without the weight of the 13-inch models.
worthKnowing: Only compatible with 1st generation Apple Pencil (sold separately, requires USB-C adapter) — the 10th gen iPad doesn't support the Apple Pencil 2 or Apple Pencil Pro, limiting drawing workflows.
64GB base storage is tight — for users who store apps, photos, and videos locally, the 256GB model ($649 CAD) is a better long-term investment.

commonComplaints.title: Apple Pencil 1 only with awkward adapter
commonComplaints.body: The iPad 10th gen supports only the 1st gen Apple Pencil, which requires a USB-C to Lightning adapter that is sold separately. It's an awkward charging solution.

commonComplaints.title: Base 64GB fills quickly
commonComplaints.body: Apps, photos, and iPadOS itself consume 20–30GB out of the box, leaving limited room. The 256GB model is the recommended starting point for regular use.

commonComplaints.title: A14 chip showing its age for demanding tasks
commonComplaints.body: The A14 Bionic from 2020 handles most tasks well but stutters in demanding games and large Procreate files compared to newer chips.

verdict: The iPad 10th generation is the right starting point for most iPad buyers — great display, USB-C, 10-hour battery, and Apple's ecosystem at $449. For drawing or heavier creative work, the Apple Pencil limitation makes the iPad mini 7 or Air a better choice. For casual users, this is the iPad to buy.

realBuyers.quote: Perfect for Netflix, YouTube, Zoom, and books. Everything I needed an iPad for. Battery is great.
Kids use it for school — durable, fast, great apps. Exactly what we needed for the price.
USB-C finally. I can use it with all my other cables now.

metaDescription: Apple iPad 10th gen review Canada: A14 Bionic, 10.9" Liquid Retina, USB-C, 64GB. $449 CAD. Best value iPad for Canadians.
